paper22.spatial_mode_ladder.label: Paper 22 Spatial Mode Ladder
paper22.spatial_mode_ladder.statement: The closed `S^3` spatial carrier decomposes into scalar, vector, and TT tensor ladders with `lambda_n^(S) = n(n+2) / a^2`, `lambda_n^(V) = (n+1)^2 / a^2`, `lambda_n^(TT,rough) = (n(n+2)-2) / a^2`, multiplicities `(n+1)^2`, `2n(n+2)`, `2(n-1)(n+3)`, and diagonal-spin floors `J_min = 0,1,2` respectively.
premises: `premise.1` and `premise.2` place the perturbation problem on the accepted IO closed-space setting.
Paper 22 constructs the scalar/vector Hodge carriers and the TT extension on round `S^3`.
proof_outline: Decompose the round-`S^3` spatial Hilbert space into scalar, coexact-vector, and TT tensor branches.
Read off the exact eigenvalues, multiplicities, and diagonal-spin supports for each branch.
Use those branch laws as the theorem-grade spatial carrier for later perturbation work.
scope_boundary: Spatial carrier only.
Does not by itself determine the dynamical perturbation/source equations.
paper22.spatial_mode_ladder.reference_note: Supporting references only. The theorem text carried on this node is the calculator's self-contained public dictionary entry.

paper23.closed_scalar_operator.label: Paper 23 Closed Scalar Operator Theorem
paper23.closed_scalar_operator.statement: On the closed scalar hierarchy, the first curvature correction is `lambda_n - 3 = (n-1)(n+3)`, scalar hyperspherical support obeys `ell = 0,1,...,n`, and the physical inhomogeneous scalar spectrum begins at `n >= 2` with `n = 1` pure gauge.
paper23.closed_scalar_operator.scope: Closed scalar shell operator and physical mode support.
premises: `paper22.spatial_mode_ladder` fixes the scalar harmonic carrier on `S^3`.
The closed scalar perturbation problem lives on that discrete shell basis rather than a flat continuous `k` basis.
proof_outline: Start from the scalar `S^3` harmonic ladder with `lambda_n = n(n+2)`.
Insert the closed-space scalar curvature correction to obtain `lambda_n - 3 = (n-1)(n+3)`.
Classify `n = 0` as background, `n = 1` as gauge, and `n >= 2` as physical scalar support.
scope_boundary: Closed scalar shell operator and support only.
Does not by itself close the full metric-plus-fluid source/acoustic hierarchy.
paper23.closed_scalar_operator.reference_note: Supporting references only. The theorem text carried on this node is the calculator's self-contained public dictionary entry.

paper37.highz_tail_slaving.statement: Support above `z ~ 1500` is demoted to a slaved residual tail rather than an independent selector-bearing sector, with the reduced packet differing from the full endpoint family only by `Delta_theta = +5.386582264233e-06` and `Delta_ell = -4.390006880612e-04`.
premises: `paper37.packet_coefficient_fixing` isolates the live packet carrier near the active endpoint.
Any support above `z ~ 1500` still has to be checked for an independent selector-bearing branch.
proof_outline: Audit the continuation of the active endpoint family above the reduced packet support.
Show that the remaining high-`z` contribution is slaved to the packet carrier rather than introducing a second independent selector sector.
Demote the high-`z` continuation to a residual tail in the active-branch support decomposition.
scope_boundary: Support decomposition on the active endpoint family.
Does not by itself identify the physical selector leaf.
paper37.highz_tail_slaving.reference_note: Supporting references only. The theorem text carried on this node is the calculator's self-contained public dictionary entry.

paper37.peak_window_tail_profile_audit.label: Peak-window Tail Profile Audit
paper37.peak_window_tail_profile_audit.statement: After the best pure-amplitude rescale is removed, the residual first-peak parent-profile mismatch obeys `RMS_rel[ell in [190,250]] = 2.950052007950388e-06`, so the remaining tail is not a second active selector-bearing branch.
paper37.peak_window_tail_profile_audit.scope: First-peak TT parent-profile audit on the active endpoint family.
premises: `paper37.highz_tail_slaving` demotes the high-`z` continuation to a residual tail candidate.
The physical relevance of that tail still has to be checked against the first-peak parent profile.
proof_outline: Compare the first-peak parent profile with and without the residual tail after removing the best pure-amplitude rescale.
Measure the remaining mismatch on the peak window and show that it is tiny.
Use that audit to rule out the residual tail as a second active selector-bearing branch.
scope_boundary: First-peak TT parent-profile audit on the active endpoint family.
Verified only on the relevant first-peak window, not as a full-spectrum theorem.
paper37.peak_window_tail_profile_audit.reference_note: Supporting references only. The theorem text carried on this node is the calculator's self-contained public dictionary entry.

paper37.selector_support_promotion.label: Selector-support Promotion Theorem
paper37.selector_support_promotion.claim_status: derived / scoped plus verified / carried selector closure
paper37.selector_support_promotion.statement: The support-certified cumulative packet `z_cross < 1500` carries the physical selector leaf on the active branch, fixing `z_sel = 1092.267038673162`.
premises: `paper37.packet_coefficient_fixing` provides the support-reduced active carrier.
`paper37.highz_tail_slaving` and `paper37.peak_window_tail_profile_audit` remove the residual tail as an independent selector branch.
proof_outline: Promote the cumulative packet `z_cross < 1500` from a reduced support object to the carried physical selector carrier on the active branch.
Collapse the old endpoint-family selector interval to the leaf transported by that certified support packet.
Feed the carried leaf back into the Paper 36 selector backbone as the physical active-branch leaf.
scope_boundary: Carried active-branch physical selector leaf only.
Does not establish a universal selector-promotion theorem off branch.
paper37.selector_support_promotion.reference_note: Supporting references only. The theorem text carried on this node is the calculator's self-contained public dictionary entry.

paper37.active_branch_theta_star.scope: Fixed active Paper 10 legacy projected branch only.
premises: `paper37.selector_support_promotion` fixes the carried physical selector leaf on the active branch.

proof_outline: Evaluate the exact strict-bare selector backbone at the carried active selector leaf.
Obtain `theta_bare`, transport to `theta_obs` with the fixed Jacobian `J_theta`, and report `100theta_*` for the active branch.
Check the same carried solution against the direct first TT peak observable so the derived number remains tied to the measured peak position.

Theorem-grade numeric closure on the carried selector leaf only.
Not a universal off-branch transfer theorem or a full TT/TE/EE solver closure.

papers.summary: Derives the complete IO Friedmann equation, combining the matter and bare-Λ sector with the energy projection √Δ and the curvature projection Δ^(-1/x²). The paper presents the framework's first full holographic background equation with zero free parameters and makes the modular-energy role of the projection factors explicit.

Derives AC1 from the existing Paper 15–20 stack and upgrades the key acoustic theorem from Paper 20 to derived status within the reduced scalar acoustic sector. It also isolates the SU(2) Geometric Weight as a candidate explanation for the remaining radiation-response deficit in deuterium.

Proposes IO-native replacements for ΛCDM-borrowed inputs in the framework's confrontation with Planck 2018 CMB data. The headline result is a conditional Hawking-boundary prediction for the primordial scalar amplitude, A_s = 2.007 × 10⁻⁹, and the paper also shows that clustering-sector baryon quantities do not belong in CMB perturbation slots.

Presents the first fully IO-native CLASS Boltzmann-code confrontation with Planck 2018 TT data and diagnoses the residual mismatch as an architectural conflict between the IO boundary-projective multi-baryon framework and CLASS's single-baryon bulk simulator. The paper promotes multiple theorem candidates, corrects source-side perturbation ingredients, and audits the full scalar-amplitude normalization chain.

Rebuilds the confrontation layer on the active Paper 10 legacy branch, retires the mixed-branch package, and closes the branch self-consistency audit. On the active legacy package, cosmic chronometers give χ² = 14.70 and the derived/scoped DESI DR2 GCcomb galaxy/quasar BAO route gives χ² = 27.74, for combined χ² = 42.44 versus 46.40 for the fixed-parameter Planck ΛCDM reference.

Recomputes the twenty-test confrontation on the active Paper 10 legacy branch. Pantheon+ and TDCOSMO still favor IO over the fixed-parameter Planck reference, the Alcock-Paczyński test still favors Planck, and several mixed-branch headline numbers change materially on the rebuilt branch, especially in the late-structure sector.

Recomputes the Weyl-response and late-time observable map on the active Paper 10 legacy branch. The current branch gives Σ_IO = 0.8114, S₈,weyl = 0.8339, α_Φ ≈ 2.005 in the E_G pipeline, and a derived/scoped galaxy/quasar BAO closure on the current DESI DR2 GCcomb definition.

papers.short_title: Closure: The Complete Interior Observer Framework
papers.summary: Paper 32 closes the six remaining open problems on their active scoped sectors, retires all four semiclassical principles on their stated scopes, and carries the late-time chain through x_crit, hidden-support recollapse, and the Paper 32 closure/lifecycle program. This card reflects the current Zenodo title/description while the site’s branch-sensitive numerics follow the active Paper 10 legacy reconstruction.

papers.short_title: Four Problems, One Horizon: Baryogenesis, Impossible Galaxies, Dark Energy, and the Dark Matter Null from Inside a Schwarzschild Horizon
papers.summary: Paper 35 packages four confrontation layers on top of the active Paper 10 legacy branch. It closes the late-time baryon-to-photon ratio route, sharpens the BBN bulk-frame η route, reduces baryogenesis to an exact topological obstruction on the current stack, shows that the IO master clock gives 46-48% more formation time than Planck ΛCDM at z > 10, corrects the observer-side dark-energy mapping away from the retired w = -1/3 carryover, and frames continuing direct-detection null results as the expected outcome of the geometric dark-sector reading.
